name: Bohemian Braid
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the water, yeast, milk, sugar, salt, eggs, shortening, half the flour, lemon rind, and mace in a large bowl.
2. Gradually add the remaining flour until the dough is smooth and workable.
3. Knead on a floured surface until the dough is smooth and elastic.
4. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turn to coat, cover with a damp tea towel, and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
5. Punch down the dough. Divide into two portions.
6. Set one portion aside. Divide the other portion into four equal parts.
7. Shape three of the pieces into 14-inch long strands and arrange them on a lightly greased cookie sheet (diagonally works well). Secure the ends under the braids.
8. Take the remaining piece, divide it into thirds, and shape into a second smaller braid to place on top of the first.
9. Repeat with the other half of dough on a second greased cookie sheet.
10. Loosely cover each braid with damp tea towels and let rise for 45 minutes to an hour.
11. For decoration, gently press halved maraschino cherries (well rinsed) or pecan halves into the joints of the braids.
12. Brush the loaves with a glaze made from egg yolk and 2 tablespoons water.
13. Bake at 350°F for 30-40 minutes, or until golden brown but not dark. Cool completely before serving.
Recipe By: Betty Crocker, 1956
Serving Size: 12
Preparation Time: 1 hour
